commands.review_summary:
The PR adds pre-provider-normalization quarantine for non-serializable assistant runtime tool schemas and tests that healthy sibling tools still reach session creation.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +153. Total +235 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main passes raw tools into provider normalization before the runtime-compatible projection, so a non-serializable schema can reach provider traversal before quarantine. I did not execute a failing runtime scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with compatibility semantics; no concrete code defect was found, so the next action is human maintainer acceptance rather than automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid runtime tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

Pruned the runtime tool-schema quarantine branch down to the assistant-turn failure path.

This adds a pre-normalization quarantine for active runtime tools whose parameter schema cannot be serialized safely before provider normalization. Healthy sibling tools continue into provider normalization, and the existing runtime-compatible projection still filters model/runtime-incompatible schemas before session creation.

The test fixtures use neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed on head `206d9a7d3d0`: 3 files, 64 tests, 18.86s.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Diff scrub for private paths, install-error output, and the originally reported fixture name passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` returned clean before the latest rebase: no accepted/actionable findings.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed before current-main drift: run `run_2a737c0d190e`, lease `cbx_7680f04b0f2d` / `crimson-crayfish`, provider `aws`, command 3m11s, total 5m16s.
- Current `pnpm check:changed` completion is blocked by unrelated current-main production type errors on `origin/main` `096bd13962c`; latest reproduced blocker is AWS Crabbox run `run_4585fd8a05d3`, lease `cbx_e38c00acc044` / `golden-lobster`, provider `aws`.
- Draft unblocker PR #88533 now carries only the remaining current-main test-fixture cleanup after rebase; it does not change this runtime patch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A single active tool with a schema that cannot survive provider/runtime schema projection is quarantined before assistant session creation instead of crashing the whole assistant turn.

Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; remote Linux changed gate through AWS Crabbox before current-main drift.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Verification above.

Evidence after fix: Focused runner coverage shows session creation receives only the healthy sibling tool when one active tool has a non-serializable schema; projection/quarantine unit coverage verifies provider-repairable schemas are preserved and non-serializable schemas are reported.

Observed result after fix: Healthy tools continue; the bad tool is reported through the existing trusted diagnostic/quarantine path.

What was not tested: Current `pnpm check:changed` completion is blocked by unrelated current-main production type errors listed in `run_4585fd8a05d3`. The runtime schema patch had a passing AWS changed gate before that drift and has focused proof on the latest pushed head.
target.author: vincentkoc
